text: Here's what science actually says about type 1 vs type 2 diabetes. Type 1 diabetes is an autoimmune condition, where the body attacks insulin-producing cells. But type 2 diabetes? That's primarily about insulin resistance, not destruction. Both conditions can lead to serious health issues, but the management differs greatly. Research shows that while type 1 is often diagnosed in childhood, type 2 is on the rise in adults and children alike. Understanding these differences is crucial for prevention and treatment. Curious to learn more? Hit that follow button for more insights!
